notes: price-cache is a read-through cache between catalog-svc and pricing-db. Deploy v9f1c5a7 changed the cache key format (added a tenant/currency segment), so existing warm entries no longer matched and the hit ratio collapsed from ~94% to ~6%. Every catalog read now misses and falls through to a slow pricing-db lookup, so price-cache fill latency exploded and it stampedes pricing-db. catalog-svc (its direct caller) backs up waiting on price-cache fills, and api-edge at the customer edge times out and pages (loudest). pricing-db looks busy -- query QPS and active connections spike from the miss stampede -- but it stays status OK with only elevated load, it is NOT the origin (it is a victim of the stampede). The two traps: api-edge is loudest+edge, and pricing-db is the deepest component and looks saturated; the true origin is the MID-CHAIN cache whose hit ratio dropped FIRST. inventory-svc errored and just deployed (decoy, off the price read path). search-svc is a healthy control that also reads pricing-db but not via price-cache.
_design_note: Paged on api-edge because that is the customer-facing storefront edge where latency is measured. Breaks both deepest=origin (pricing-db) and edge=origin (api-edge): the origin is the cache in the middle.
